Comment: Helicopter on a RealFlight heli model. The "px4" values are HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS.controls[] indices and MUST match PWM_MAIN_FUNC* in ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d-posix/airframes/1203_flightaxis_heli. CA_AIRFRAME 11 (tail servo) orders the allocator motors first, then servos: controls[0]=main rotor motor [0,1], [1]=yaw tail servo [-1,1], [2..4]=swash plate servos 1-3 [-1,1].
DemixComment: HeliDemix converts the three swash servo outputs on rf0-2 back into the roll/pitch/collective triple RealFlight expects: roll=(s1-s2)/1.732, pitch=((s1+s2)/2-s3)/1.5, col=(s1+s2+s3)/3, recentered to 0..1. The two divisors are NOT cosmetic - the raw differences carry gain 0.866 and 0.75 about the 0.5 centre where a full command needs 0.5, so without them roll is over-driven 1.732x and pitch 1.5x and RealFlight cyclic saturates at only +/-0.577 and +/-0.667 of command. Collective already has gain 0.5 and is left alone. The inverse is only exact for a swash geometry with servos 1 and 2 mirrored about the X axis and servo 3 on the axis, which is why the airframe overrides CA_SP0_ANG* to 300/60/180 instead of the PX4 defaults 0/140/220 - and the divisors follow from those angles, so changing one without the other breaks the decoupling.
DisarmComment: rf0-2 each carry an explicit "disarm" of 0.5, the neutral swash position, matching the explicit "disarm" every motor row already states. Omitting it would default to -1 = "hold last output". That is safe as the bridge stands - buildChannels() demixes out of a scratch copy and writes only out[], leaving the persistent channels[] untransformed, so a held slot holds a raw servo value and re-running the demix on it is idempotent. It was NOT safe before that split. HeliDemix used to be applied in place on channels[] every frame, so a held slot came back round already demixed and was demixed again: a non-involutive linear map that from a plausible in-flight swash (0.8, 0.3, 0.5) went chaotic and railed within ~3 frames at ~250 Hz. Neutral (0.5, 0.5, 0.5) is a fixed point, so pre-arm looked clean and only the armed->disarmed transition with a deflected swash tripped it - i.e. every normal landing - with nothing logged. get_FAbridge_params.py still rejects a hold-last row on any slot an option rewrites, and these rows still state their disarm value, because the property that makes hold-last safe lives in one function in another file and nothing here would notice if it were lost again. The same disarm path also runs if any mapped control arrives NaN while armed.
YawComment: rf3 is bipolar: under CA_AIRFRAME 11 the tail is a SERVO, so PWMSim normalises it to [-1,1] and 0.5 is zero tail pitch. That matches the collective-pitch tail rotor nearly every stock RealFlight single-rotor heli models. CA_AIRFRAME 10 would make the tail a motor clamped to [0,1], which clips every left-yaw command - measured against the bridge: yaw -3 rad/s produced rf3=0.000 and the tail idled on its lower clip, while type 11 gives 0.130 / 0.548 / 1.000 for -3 / neutral / +3.
RfModel: informational only - aircraft is selected inside RealFlight

Channels.comment: tail rotor pitch / yaw <- controls[1] yaw tail servo
